response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software craftsman with a strong background in web and distributed systems, but his career is overwhelmingly centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than C++. His only documented C++ work amounts to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project—far below the depth required for a senior C++ architect role. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems design in C++, familiarity with industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443 secure development practices, or IoT/industrial automation contexts. The role's core technical requirements are almost entirely unmet.
strengths: Broad distributed and event-driven architecture experience, including a self-built Geo-Data platform on Kubernetes
Demonstrated teaching and mentoring culture through workshops, code katas, and Selenium courses
Solid Agile/TDD/CI/CD discipline practiced across multiple employers
concerns: C++ expertise is negligible—only ~6 months of professional use and a thesis project over a 15+ year career dominated by web technologies
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443 standards, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices
No experience with MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or IoT/industrial automation domains

key_factors.factor: No industrial security or IEC 62443 experience
Strong software craftsmanship and architecture practices
